title: Memory-aware split policy for multi-module softmax-tail mapping
hypothesis: Introduce a bounded mapper policy that compares monolithic and row-split lowering for multi-module softmax-tail execution and chooses the lower-overhead option using simple sync/tail-cost signals.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does the bounded mapper change materially improve nm2 on the existing softmaxcmp hardware?
include: fp16_nm2_softmax_r4 old-mapper baseline from the prior fused-output run
fp16_nm2_softmax_r4 new-mapper candidate on the same hardware
exclude: fp16_nm1_softmax_r4 during the first-stage mapper proof
global architecture ranking across nm1 and nm2 in the first evaluation
follow_on_broad_sweep: re-run nm1 and nm2 together only after the local nm2 mapper effect is established or remains ambiguous

expected_benefit: fairer evaluation of fp16_nm2_softmax_r4 on existing softmaxcmp hardware
lower synchronization overhead on tiny softmax-tail workloads
clearer separation between architecture weakness and mapper weakness
risks: the benchmark may still be too small to justify multi-module hardware even with a better mapper
a narrow heuristic may improve this workload but not generalize
expanded mapper choice can complicate campaign reproducibility if not recorded explicitly
